brand.name: Election Guide
brand.summary: Understand the election process with timelines, step-by-step guides, quizzes, and calendar planning.

features.title: Timeline View
features.desc: Follow the major phases of an election from filing to certification.

features.title: How It Works
features.desc: Walk through the core steps that shape the democratic process.

features.title: Quiz Yourself
features.desc: Check your knowledge with a short civic literacy quiz.

features.title: Calendar Planning
features.desc: Track important dates and add them to your calendar.

features.title: AI Assistant
features.desc: Get concise explanations for election topics on demand.

features.title: Accessible by Default
features.desc: Built with keyboard-friendly controls and readable contrast.

general.title: Candidate filing deadline
general.desc: Candidates submit the paperwork needed to appear on the ballot.

general.title: Voter registration opens
general.desc: Eligible voters can register or update their information before the general election.

general.title: Early voting begins
general.desc: Registered voters may cast ballots before Election Day at designated locations.

general.title: Election Day
general.desc: The main voting day when eligible voters cast their ballots in person or by mail.

general.title: Vote counting and certification
general.desc: Election officials tally ballots and certify the final results.

general.title: Electoral College meets
general.desc: Electors cast their formal votes for president and vice president.

general.title: Congress certifies results
general.desc: Congress counts and certifies the Electoral College vote.

primary.title: Party filing deadline
primary.desc: Candidates file to appear on the primary ballot and begin formal campaigning.

primary.title: Campaign season
primary.desc: Candidates travel, debate, and raise support in early voting states.

primary.title: Super Tuesday
primary.desc: A large group of states vote on the same day and reshape the race.

primary.title: Remaining state primaries
primary.desc: The rest of the states and territories choose delegates and nominees.

primary.title: Party convention
primary.desc: Delegates formally nominate the party ticket and adopt a platform.

primary.title: General election campaign
primary.desc: Nominated candidates campaign nationally before the general election.

local.title: Candidate filing period
local.desc: Local candidates file paperwork for offices like city council and school board.

local.title: Nonpartisan primary
local.desc: Many local races use an initial round to narrow the field.

local.title: Campaign and outreach
local.desc: Candidates connect with voters through events, canvassing, and public forums.

local.title: Local Election Day
local.desc: Voters choose local leaders and decide issues that affect their communities.

local.title: Results and runoff
local.desc: If required, a runoff election decides the winner between the top candidates.

steps.name: Candidate Announces
steps.detail: A prospective candidate declares their intention to run and starts building a campaign.
checklist: Register with the FEC for federal races

steps.name: Fundraising Begins
steps.detail: Campaigns raise money through donations, events, and online giving tools.

steps.name: Primary Election
steps.detail: Voters choose the nominee who will represent a party in the general election.
checklist: Check whether the primary is open or closed

steps.name: Party Convention
steps.detail: Delegates formally nominate the party ticket and announce the platform.
checklist: The vice presidential nominee is announced
The nominee gives an acceptance speech

steps.name: General Campaign
steps.detail: The nominees campaign nationally and try to persuade undecided voters.

steps.name: Election Day Voting
steps.detail: Eligible voters cast ballots for the offices and issues that appear on the ballot.
checklist: Confirm your polling place and hours

steps.name: Vote Counting
steps.detail: Election officials count ballots, review provisional ballots, and report results.

steps.name: Certification and Inauguration
steps.detail: Results are certified and the winning candidates prepare to take office.
checklist: The new term begins with inauguration

quiz.q: What is the minimum age to vote in a US federal election?
quiz.explain: The 26th Amendment set the federal voting age at 18.

opts: A group of electors who formally elect the President
The US Senate and House combined
A committee that writes election laws
quiz.explain: The Electoral College is the system used to formally elect the President.

quiz.q: What happens in a primary election?
opts: Citizens vote for the President directly
quiz.explain: A primary helps a party choose the nominee who will run in the general election.

opts: Encouraging voters to stay home on rainy days
Efforts to prevent eligible citizens from voting
quiz.explain: Voter suppression means making it harder for eligible people to vote.

quiz.q: How often are US House of Representatives members elected?
quiz.explain: House members are elected every two years.

opts: An election held during a storm
A vote to remove an official from office
A tiebreaker between the top two candidates
An early voting period before Election Day
quiz.explain: A runoff is a follow-up election when no candidate gets the needed threshold.

calendarEvents.name: Voter Registration Deadline
calendarEvents.desc: Last day to register in most states before the November election.

calendarEvents.name: Early Voting Begins
calendarEvents.desc: Early in-person voting opens at designated locations.

calendarEvents.name: Presidential Election Day
calendarEvents.desc: General Election Day for federal, state, and local offices.

calendarEvents.name: Electoral College Meets
calendarEvents.desc: Electors cast their formal Electoral College votes.

calendarEvents.name: Congress Certifies Results
calendarEvents.desc: Congress counts and certifies the Electoral College vote.

calendarEvents.name: Presidential Inauguration
calendarEvents.desc: The newly elected President is sworn into office.
